canonical_definition: The latent ability of an LLM to generate responses that are distinctively aligned with a specific persona, as opposed to a neutral or default narrative stance.

definitions.text: The latent ability of an LLM to generate responses that are distinctively aligned with a specific persona, as opposed to a neutral or default narrative stance.

relationships.evidence: Recent work formalizes this phenomenon [Caricature] through two measurable components: individuation—the extent to which a response diverges from a neutral baseline—and exaggeration—the degree to which persona-linked semantics dominate beyond topical necessity (Cheng et al., 2023). (Section 2.2)

characterization: The latent ability of an LLM to generate responses that are distinctively aligned with a specific persona, as opposed to a neutral or default narrative stance.
